candidates.why: Primary operating revenue line; matches revenue from customers and equals Total Revenue amounts.
Explicit total revenue line duplicating the same figures; included as fallback in case primary label changes over time.
Finance income is not operating revenue; kept only as a very low-priority candidate per provided list but not used in final_choice.
Explicitly non-operating; not suitable for revenue despite being in candidates list.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Represents bottom-line comprehensive income, not revenue; excluded from final_choice.

variables.notes: Revenue from contracts with customers is the main operating revenue line; Total Revenue is an equivalent aggregate. Both are provided as alternatives in priority order without summing.

candidates.why: Represents R&D-related government grant income/offsets, not cost of goods sold.
Major operating expense by nature but not clearly separable into COGS vs SG&A; including it as COGS would distort SG&A and R&D mapping.
Broad operating expense bucket, likely including SG&A; not a specific cost-of-sales line.
R&D, not COGS.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Gross (before Government Grants)
candidates.why: R&D gross expense; not COGS.
Specific overhead/operating expense, not direct production cost.
Operating rental cost; overhead rather than direct cost of goods sold.
Component of other operating expenses; overhead, not specific COGS.
Net operating result, not a pure COGS line and includes multiple components.
Financial, not operating cost of sales.
Financial, not operating cost of sales.
R&D supplemental disclosure, not COGS.
Liability account, not an income statement COGS item.

variables.notes: The income statement is presented by nature (employee benefits, depreciation, other operating expenses) without a distinct cost-of-goods-sold or cost-of-sales line. To avoid arbitrary allocation between COGS and SG&A/R&D, COGS is left empty so these costs are captured via SG&A components and R&D instead.

candidates.why: Primary bucket of operating overhead expenses; classic SG&A-type category.
Subcomponent of other operating expenses; overhead in nature and fits within SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Gross
candidates.why: Balance sheet asset, not a P&L expense; not appropriate for SG&A components.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. SG&A is not presented as a single line; expenses are by nature. Employee benefit expenses, other operating expenses, legal provisions, rental expense, and the detailed audit-/service-related lines are all overhead-type operating costs and exclude COGS, D&A totals, interest, and taxes. Totals and balancing items are intentionally excluded. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Employee benefit expenses, [Income Statement] Legal Provisions & Litigation Expenses, [Income Statement] Rental Expense, [Income Statement] Stock-Based Compensation Expense - Net of Tax - Supplemental, [Income Statement] Auditor Fees, [Income Statement] Audit-Related Fees, [Income Statement] Other Services, [Income Statement] Tax Fees

candidates.why: Represents government grant offsets, not the underlying R&D expense itself.
Explicit R&D expense line; may represent net R&D under certain presentations.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Gross (before Government Grants)
candidates.why: Best representation of gross R&D spending before grant offsets; ideal for R&D intensity measures.
Supplemental disclosure of R&D, likely reconciling to primary R&D figures; useful as fallback.

final_choice.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Gross (before Government Grants)

variables.notes: Gross R&D before government grants is used as the primary measure to avoid netting against grant income. The standard and supplemental R&D lines are included as fallbacks for years where the gross line may be missing.

candidates.why: Explicit interest expense component; good candidate for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest expense specifically on lease liabilities; also a strong candidate for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of the same lease interest; useful as backup.
Interest income, opposite sign to interest expense; not preferred but could be used in net calculations if needed.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: REVIEWED. Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities and Other Interest Expense are the main explicit interest expense lines. The broader aggregate 'Finance costs' line is added from the full sheet as a lower-priority fallback in case component lines are missing in some years. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Finance costs

candidates.row_label: Basic EPS - profit or loss attributable to equity holders
candidates.why: Earnings per share metric, not an equity balance; unsuitable for book equity.

candidates.row_label: Diluted EPS - profit or loss attributable to equity holders
candidates.why: EPS metric, not equity balance.
Component of equity (e.g., OCI reserves), not total book equity.
Total shareholders’ equity; best available proxy for book equity attributable to owners given absence of a separate NCI line.
Duplicate total equity line with identical figures; equivalent to 'Total equity'.

variables.notes: No separate line for non-controlling interests is present, so Total equity effectively represents equity attributable to owners. Only one of the duplicate lines is selected to avoid duplication.

There is no balance sheet line for minority interests or non-controlling interests; equity appears to be entirely attributable to the parent’s shareholders. MIB is therefore set to 0 via an empty selection.
